legal_definition.text: Multiple disabilities means more than one of the specified disabilities including deafblindness, which means a condition in which a person may have combination of hearing and visual impairments causing severe communication, developmental and educational problems.
legal_definition.statutory_source: Schedule to the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016

statutory_status.act: Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016

benchmark_requirement.definition: Person with benchmark disability means a person with not less than forty per cent of a specified disability as certified by the certifying authority.
benchmark_requirement.composite_assessment_note: Where multiple disabilities coexist, combined disability percentage may be assessed as per notified guidelines.

reservation_category.grouping_note: Residual reservation category covering multiple disabilities including deafblindness.
reservation_category.eligibility_condition: Reservation available only to persons with benchmark disability.
reservation_category.establishment_scope: Applicable to Government establishments as defined under the Act.

certification_workflow.assessment_guidelines: Medical board assessment including evaluation of each disability component under Section 56 guidelines.

legal_rights_triggered.reasonable_accommodation: Communication support, tactile interpretation, assistive technology
legal_rights_triggered.high_support_needs: Section 38 (particularly relevant due to combined impairments)

distinction_framework: Multiple disabilities is a distinct statutory category.
Deafblindness is explicitly included within this category.
It is not merely simultaneous listing; it is legally recognized as a combined impairment condition.
Reservation grouping does not dissolve individual statutory identities.
legal_boundary_conditions: Reservation applicable only to persons with benchmark disability.
Section 34 applies only to Government establishments.
Certification valid throughout India unless lawfully revoked.
Assessment must follow notified composite disability evaluation norms.
interpretive_notes: Deafblindness is a dual sensory impairment requiring specialized communication systems.
Combined disabilities may require higher support frameworks under Section 38.
This category ensures individuals with overlapping impairments are not excluded from reservation structure.

legal_schema.law_name: Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act
